channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Storm-triggered floods in Philippines spark frantic calls to rescue trapped people, some on roofs
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Torrential rain set off by an approaching tropical storm swamped eastern Philippine cities and towns overnight in widespread flooding that trapped people, some on their roofs, and sparked frantic appeals for rescue boats and trucks, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Villagers are wary of plans to dam a river to ensure Panama Canal's water supply
item.description: EL JOBO, Panama (AP) — A long, wooden boat puttered down the Indio River’s chocolate waters carrying Ana María Antonio and a colleague from the Panama Canal Authority on a mission to hear directly from villagers who could be affected by plans to dam the river.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A gasoline truck overturns and explodes near Uganda's capital, killing 11
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — At least 11 people were killed Tuesday after a fuel truck exploded next to a highway in Uganda, police said. Two children were among the dead.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sanders leads call for DOJ to investigate Israeli attack on journalists
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) and 11 Democrats are urging President Biden, Secretary of State Antony Blinken and Attorney General Merrick Garland to open a formal investigation into an Israeli air strike that injured American journalists over a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Storm-triggered floods in Philippines spark frantic calls to rescue trapped people, some on roofs
item.description: Torrential rain set off by an approaching storm has swamped eastern Philippine cities and towns in widespread floods that trapped people, some on their roofs, and sparked frantic appeals for rescue boats and trucks.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: About 140 Rohingya Muslims on wooden boat off Indonesia's coast as residents refuse to let them land
item.description: LABUHAN HAJI, Indonesia (AP) — About 140 weak and hungry Rohingya Muslims, mostly women and children, were on a wooden boat anchored about 1 mile (0.60 kilometers) off the coast of Indonesia’s northernmost province of Aceh on Tuesday, officials said, and local residents refused to allow them onto la
item.dc:creator: BINSAR BAKKARA and EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cuba fights to come back from a nationwide blackout, then a storm that killed six
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — A small town in far eastern Cuba was recovering Tuesday from flooding that killed at least six people after Oscar crossed the island’s eastern coast as a tropical storm with winds and heavy rain.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ and MILEXSY DURAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The little airline that could — Lebanon's national carrier braves Israeli airstrikes
item.description: Since Israel began bombarding Beirut’s southern suburbs in its war against Hezbollah militants, Lebanon’s national air carrier has become a local icon simply by continuing to do its job.
item.dc:creator: Abby Sewell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: It could take 350 years for Gaza to rebuild if it remains under a blockade, UN report says
item.description: United Nations agencies have long warned that it could take decades to rebuild Gaza after Israel’s offensive against Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Krauss, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France defends restrictions on Israeli firms supplying Middle East wars and says it's not a boycott
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France's government on Tuesday defended its decision to bar Israeli companies supplying the wars in the Middle East from exhibiting at an upcoming trade fair outside Paris. Organizers of the Nov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A gasoline truck overturns and explodes near Uganda's capital, killing 11
item.description: Police in Uganda say least 11 people including children were killed when a fuel truck exploded next to a highway on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Muhumuza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine blasts UN chief over potential meeting with Putin at BRICS, says stayed away from Kyiv's peace summit
item.description: Ukraine blasted U.N. Secretary-General Guterres for allegedly agreeing to meet with Russian President Putin during the BRICS summit as leaders from the West's top adversaries like China and Iran convene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI says it's investigating unauthorized release of documents on Israel's possible attack plans
item.description: The FBI says it's investigating the unauthorized release of classified documents on Israel’s preparation for a potential retaliatory attack on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says only a US green light on a NATO invitation will sway a reluctant Germany
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's president is hoping allies will take a more positive stance on his so-called “victory plan” after the U.S. election, but concedes that its key demand — an invitation to join NATO — is not welcome by some major Western partners, in particular Germany.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mystery of downed airplane in Sudan deepens as Kyrgyzstan insists aircraft had been de-registered
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The mystery surrounding a crashed cargo plane in Sudan purportedly downed by the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces deepened Tuesday as authorities insisted the aircraft had been de-registered in Kyrgyzstan.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah naval base in Beirut as Iran rallies Gulf Arab nations
item.description: Hezbollah launched missiles on central Israel Tuesday. Meanwhile, Israel Defense Forces said it struck a Hezbollah naval base in Beirut, along with other terrorist targets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tropical Storm Oscar swirls toward the Bahamas after hitting Cuba as a hurricane
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Oscar chugged toward the Bahamas on Tuesday after making landfall in Cuba as a Category 1 hurricane, killing at least six people as it unleashed heavy rains on an island also hit by an unrelated massive power outage.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Peru’s ex-president Toledo gets more than 20 years in prison in case linked to corruption scandal
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s former President Alejandro Toledo on Monday was sentenced to 20 years and six months in prison in a case involving Brazilian construction giant Odebrecht, which became synonymous with corruption across Latin America, where it paid millions of dollars in bribes to government
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Samoan coast where King Charles will visit worries about the future after ship damaged reef
item.description: SIUMU, Samoa (AP) — Under clear skies Tuesday, shortly after sunrise in the beachside fishing village of Siumu, Samoa, a dozen men and children prepared their small boats for a day at sea.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and AYAKA McGILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel says it killed a Hezbollah official expected to become the group's next leader
item.description: The Israeli military says one of its airstrikes in early October killed a top Hezbollah official who had been widely expected to become the group’s next leader.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In annual religious pilgrimage, Panamanians crawl to reach the Black Christ of Portobelo
item.description: PORTOBELO, Panama (AP) — At dawn, pilgrims like Ernesto Troya undertook the hardest stretch of their journey to reach the statue of the Black Christ of Portobelo in Panama. Moved by faith, they crawled for several hours along the street toward the image of the saint.
item.dc:creator: MATÍAS DELACROIX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds mourn Catholic priest and Indigenous peace activist killed in southern Mexico
item.description: SAN ANDRES LARRAINZAR, Mexico (AP) — Hundreds gathered Monday to mourn Catholic priest Marcelo Pérez, an activist for Indigenous peoples and farm laborers who was killed in the southern Mexico state of Chiapas.
item.dc:creator: RAÚL VERA and ISABEL MATEOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken heads to Middle East in wake of Sinwar's killing
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is going to the Middle East in the wake of the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Voting from abroad: What to know
item.description: Most U.S. citizens who reside outside the country can vote absentee in primary and general elections.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah generates funds for activities under the guise of a non-profit: Israeli officials
item.description: IDF spokesperson Daniel Hagari revealed that Hezbollah uses a charity under the name Al-Qard Al-Hasan in order to fund terrorist activities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tropical Storm Oscar dumps heavy rain on Cuba as it heads toward the Bahamas
item.description: Its maximum sustained winds blew at 40 mph on Monday as Oscar moved west-northwest at 3 mph. The storm was located about 15 miles (25 kilometers) west-northwest of Guantánamo, Cuba, the U.S. National Hurricane Center in Miami said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sanders: Trump will be 'closer to Netanyahu' on Gaza if reelected
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said Sunday he believes former President Trump will have similar views of the Israel-Hamas war to those of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu if he is reelected.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Reddit user discovers mysterious 'doorway' in Antarctica
item.description: "Monsters Inc." bedroom doors, and an underground bunker were more suggested explanations, some in jest others not.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu promises retaliation following assassination attempt by Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned Hezbollah that its alleged attempt to assassinate him and his wife was a "grave mistake.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Funeral home in Poland apologizes after a corpse falls out of a hearse in traffic
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — A funeral home in Poland issued an unusual apology on Saturday after a corpse that it was transporting fell out of a hearse and into traffic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Harvard donations drop; alums cut ties over anti-Israel protests
item.description: Harvard, the nation's richest university, saw donations plunge 15% after campus protests, new report shows.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, The Hill

item.title: Israel says drone launched toward Netanyahu's home from Lebanon
item.description: The office of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said early Saturday that a drone was launched from Lebanon toward Netanyahu's home in Caesarea. Netanyahu and his wife were not home at the time of the incident and no casualties were reported, according to The Associated Press (AP).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah says its war with Israel is entering a new phase
item.description: Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group said Friday it is entering a new phase in its fight against invading Israeli troops, as the region reckons with the killing of top Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in a battle with Israeli forces in Gaza. Hamas acknowledged Sinwar’s death and described him as a martyr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea troops readying for Ukraine combat: South Korea
item.description: South Korea said it's used facial recognition AI tech to identify North Korean officers in eastern Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ju-min Park, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: South Korea condemned the North's constitutional revision as “an anti-unification, anti-national act.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation
